menstrual.phase_summary: Your period's here — a natural time to rest, restore, and listen closely to your body.

plan: A slow, easy walk outdoors if you feel up to it
Light mobility work — hips, lower back, shoulders
Breathing exercises or a short meditation
workout.avoid: Skip high-intensity training and heavy lifting this week — your body's energy is focused elsewhere.

nutrition.tip: Pair iron-rich foods like palak or rajma with vitamin C — citrus or tomatoes — to help your body absorb more of it.

follicular.phase_summary: Energy's rising — a great window to build strength and push a little harder.

plan: Strength training with progressive overload, 3-4 sets
Try a new class or workout style — motivation tends to run high
Active recovery walk or light cardio on off days
workout.avoid: No major restrictions — just build up intensity gradually rather than maxing out on day one.

nutrition.focus: Fueling muscle repair and rising energy needs
nutrition.tip: Protein at each meal — dahi, paneer, or chana — supports the muscle-building your body's primed for right now.

ovulatory.phase_summary: You're at your peak — strength, coordination, and energy tend to be at their best.

plan: Your hardest training days — heavy lifts or high-intensity intervals
A good window to try a personal best if you've been building toward one
Group classes or partner workouts — social energy tends to be high
workout.avoid: Don't skip your warm-up even on high-energy days — ligaments are slightly more flexible now, so joints need extra care.

nutrition.focus: Supporting peak energy and hormonal balance
nutrition.tip: Healthy fats like til and flaxseeds support hormone production during this phase — a small daily handful goes a long way.

luteal.phase_summary: Energy's winding down and cravings may pick up — a good week to ease off and listen in.

plan: Lower the intensity — moderate cardio or lighter weights
Shorter sessions are okay — consistency matters more than intensity now
An extra rest day if you're feeling low on energy
workout.avoid: This isn't the week to chase a new personal best — pushing too hard now often backfires.

nutrition.focus: Steadying blood sugar and easing cravings
nutrition.tip: Cravings for something sweet are common now — dates or a square of dark chocolate can satisfy it without a big sugar crash.
